_comment: Econur Recommendation Engine Config. Controls how skin types map to products and how specific answers can override the default mapping. To change a mapping: edit primaryMapping. To add an override rule: add an entry to answerOverrides. No JS changes needed.

primaryMapping._comment: Default product per skin type. Evaluated only after all answerOverrides have been checked. FIXED: D used to point to the old 'dry-skin' id, which was actually the Licorice Brightening Bar (a normal/combination brightening formula, not a dry-skin formula) — D now correctly points to Olivelle, the real dry/sensitive-skin product. X used to share that same mislabeled id; it now correctly points to 'licorice-brightening', whose actual purpose (fading dark spots, pigmentation, dullness) matches X directly.

answerOverrides._comment: If user's self-reported primary concern is acne AND computed type is Normal or Combination → upgrade to Active Defense. Self-report carries high weight and overrides a borderline N/C result.
answerOverrides.reason: Self-reported acne as primary concern overrides balanced/normal recommendation

answerOverrides._comment: If user reports frequent widespread acne (q4) AND computed type is Combination → Active Defense. Frequent acne dominates the combination pattern.
answerOverrides.reason: Frequent acne in combination skin → Active Defense more appropriate than Herbifresh

answerOverrides._comment: NEW. If skin tone reads shiny/oily (q6) AND computed type is Combination → Caffiend. Caffiend's coffee formula is documented specifically for combination + mildly oily skin, with extra oil control and exfoliation that the general-purpose Herbifresh bar doesn't target as directly. Placed after the acne overrides above so a self-reported or frequent acne signal takes priority over a plain oiliness signal when both are present — this assumes the engine evaluates answerOverrides in array order and applies the first match; please confirm that matches your implementation.
answerOverrides.reason: Visible shine/oiliness on combination skin → Caffiend's coffee-based oil control fits better than the general Herbifresh bar

answerOverrides._comment: NEW. If winter brings 'slightly less oily, mostly fine' (q8), the underlying oily+combination signal still applies in non-winter months → Caffiend over Herbifresh, same reasoning as above.
answerOverrides.reason: Oily/combination seasonal pattern → Caffiend fits the oil-control need better than the general Herbifresh bar

_comment_removed_overrides: Removed 'override_sensitive_dry_q5burn' and 'override_winter_sensitive_dry' (both previously pointed D → olivelle). They're now redundant no-ops since D's primaryMapping already resolves to olivelle by default — keeping dead config that never changes the outcome was itself a small source of confusion, so they were dropped rather than left in place.
_comment_priority: When two skin types score equally, the one listed first in tieBreakingPriority wins. Order: clinical risk first (A, S), then most common types, then N as catch-all.
_comment_threshold: Secondary skin type is shown only if its score is at least 65% of the primary type's score. Prevents showing a secondary type when it's only a weak signal.
_comment_fallback: If primaryMapping has no entry for a computed skin type, fall back to this product ID.
_comment_himalayan: Himalayan Pink Salt Soap is intentionally excluded from this recommendation engine and from products.json entirely — it is not a candidate outcome for any skin type or override in this quiz.

whatsappMessageTemplates._comment: WhatsApp order/inquiry message templates. {{skinTypeName}} and {{productName}} are interpolated at runtime.

standard.bn: হ্যালো Econur! আমার ত্বক '{{skinTypeName}}'। আমি {{productName}} অর্ডার করতে চাই।
standard.en: Hello Econur! My skin type is '{{skinTypeName}}'. I'd like to order the {{productName}}.

custom.bn: হ্যালো Econur! আমার ত্বক '{{skinTypeName}}'। কাস্টম বার সম্পর্কে জানতে চাই।
custom.en: Hello Econur! My skin type is '{{skinTypeName}}'. I'd like to learn more about the Custom Bar.
